name: Quantum Computing Inc.
summary: Quantum Computing Inc. (QCi) is a Delaware-incorporated company based in Hoboken, New Jersey that uses integrated photonics and non-linear quantum optics to develop machines for quantum computing, machine learning, remote sensing, imaging, and cybersecurity. Its flagship product is the Entropy Quantum Computer (EQC), which operates at room temperature. QCi also develops thin film lithium niobate (TFLN) photonic integrated circuits and acquired Luminar Semiconductor Inc. in February 2026 to expand photonic chip and aerospace/defense capabilities.

products.name: Entropy Quantum Computer (EQC)
products.description: Room-temperature quantum computer using photonic loop architecture to solve complex optimization problems via controlled feedback through energy loss.

products.name: TFLN Optical Chips
products.description: Thin film lithium niobate photonic integrated circuits for use in QCi product lines and sale into commercial optical device markets.

products.name: Qatalyst
products.description: Software platform developed before the QPhoton Merger, integrated with QCi's photonics products.

products.name: Quantum LIDAR
products.description: Quantum sensing application using pulsed laser light for distance measurement, in early commercialization stages.

products.name: Reservoir Computing
products.description: Form of neural network leveraging core photonics technology for machine learning applications, in early commercialization.

products.name: Quantum Cyber Solution
products.description: Method for highly secure communication within a network using quantum photonics technology, in early commercialization.

products.name: LSI Photonic Semiconductor Products
products.description: Chip-scale devices including laser diodes, semiconductor optical amplifiers, avalanche photodiodes, and photonic integrated circuits from acquired Luminar Semiconductor Inc.

partnerships.description: QCi leases space within ASU's Research Park in Tempe, Arizona for its TFLN chip R&D, prototyping, and small-batch manufacturing facility.

origin.chain: SEC.gov PEM → origin.rootz.global extraction → GitHub mirror
